title: Within-lap LightGBM ranker blended with classifier
summary: AUC rewards ordering, and the real decision is partly relative: who looks most urgent to pit on this lap versus other cars in the same race-lap context.
rationale: AUC rewards ordering, and the real decision is partly relative: who looks most urgent to pit on this lap versus other cars in the same race-lap context. LightGBM’s ranking objectives are designed exactly for grouped ordering problems and can complement binary probability models.
implementation_hint: Create groups by (Year, Race, LapNumber). Train `LGBMRanker` with `rank_xendcg` or `lambdarank`, label 1 for `PitNextLap` and 0 otherwise, then blend its OOF rank-normalized scores with the main classifier via simple rank averaging or a tiny stacker trained on race-group OOF predictions.
expected_effect: Improves local ordering among contemporaneous cars, which may raise ROC AUC even if absolute probabilities are not better calibrated.
risk: Some groups may have zero or multiple pitters, so ranking signal can be sparse; use it as a specialist blend, not a full replacement.
